2604.09502.title: Strategic Algorithmic Monoculture:Experimental Evidence from Coordination Games
2604.09502.paper_summary: AI agents increasingly operate in multi-agent environments where outcomes depend on coordination. We distinguish primary algorithmic monoculture -- baseline action similarity -- from strategic algorithmic monoculture, whereby agents adjust similarity in response to incentives. We implement a simple experimental design that cleanly separates these forces, and deploy it on human and large language model (LLM) subjects. LLMs exhibit high levels of baseline similarity (primary monoculture) and, like humans, they regulate it in response to coordination incentives (strategic monoculture). While LLMs coordinate extremely well on similar actions, they lag behind humans in sustaining heterogeneity when divergence is rewarded.
2604.09502.paper_summary_zh: AI 代理人越來越多地在多代理環境中運作，結果依賴於協調。我們將主要的算法單一文化——基線行動相似性——與戰略算法單一文化區分開來，後者是指代理人根據激勵調整相似性。我們實施了一個簡單的實驗設計，清晰地分離這些力量，並將其應用於人類和大型語言模型（LLM）受試者。LLM 顯示出高水平的基線相似性（主要單一文化），並且與人類一樣，根據協調激勵來調節這一點（戰略單一文化）。雖然 LLM 在相似行動上協調得非常好，但在獎勵分歧時，它們在維持異質性方面卻落後於人類。
2604.09502.author: Gonzalo Ballestero et.al.
2604.09502.authors: Gonzalo Ballestero, Hadi Hosseini, Samarth Khanna, Ran I. Shorrer
